community.video_title: She Threw Coffee at a Cop... And Started a Revolution!
community.author: @ryr1974
community.original: Yeh they didn't like it if we hung out at the Dennies after clubbing either...[long historical comment]...I love the videos though
community.draft: You're clearly deep in this history and these are real nuances worth raising. The sourcing around Compton's is genuinely patchy and Stryker has acknowledged some of those gaps herself. We try to stick close to what's documented while being honest about what's still debated. Thanks for the close read and for watching. 📚

community.video_title: She Threw Coffee at a Cop... And Started a Revolution!
community.author: @canopusstar5157
community.original: Thank you for the history and the wonderful portraits. This is so important to recognize...
community.draft: Thank you for this. The thread of resilience running through all of this history really does feel alive right now. We get through this moment like so many others before us. Sending all that love right back. 💙

community.video_title: The Nazis Burned 100 Years of Queer Knowledge
community.author: @failtolawl
community.draft: That honestly made my day too! Thanks for watching. 😊

community.video_title: The Nazis Burned 100 Years of Queer Knowledge
community.author: @JesusChrist-sm4bm
community.original: And people said to this day that it was child porn the Nazi were good guys when every single historian...
community.draft: You laid that out perfectly. The recycling of the same framing across different eras is one of the most striking things you see when you study this history closely. Thanks for adding this. 🙌

community.video_title: One Vote. That's All That Saved New Zealand's Gay Rights Bill.
community.author: @lilykatmoon4508
community.original: Glad they've found their way back to acceptance ❤
community.draft: New Zealand's journey on this is genuinely remarkable. One vote away from a very different story. Thanks for watching. 💙

community.video_title: One Vote. That's All That Saved New Zealand's Gay Rights Bill.
community.author: @gregmcfarland57
community.draft: Thank you! One vote really was all it took. So glad this found you. 🙏

community.video_title: The Night Harvey Milk Was Assassinated, a Chorus Sang
community.author: @DashingPartyCrasher
community.original: Beautiful history lesson, as always! The other night I watched PBS's 1997 documentary about the history of the Castro...
community.draft: The PBS Castro documentary is such a gem. And Harvey as a photographer is a part of the story that doesn't get nearly enough attention. Those images are so personal and alive. Thanks for sharing your SF memories. The Castro in that era sounds like it was truly something. 📸

community.video_title: The Night Harvey Milk Was Assassinated, a Chorus Sang
community.author: @jons.6216
community.original: I remember that sad time! I was still closeted being in highschool, but noticed my San Jose school was flying the flags at half staff.
community.draft: That image of watching those flags from the closet really gets to me. It meant something even then, before you could name why. Thank you for sharing that memory. You're part of this history too. 💙

heart_replies.video_title: One Vote. That's All That Saved New Zealand's Gay Rights Bill.
heart_replies.author: @georgebaz6945

bridge.video_title: She Threw Coffee at a Cop... And Started a Revolution!
bridge.author: @osmaelias
bridge.original: This is yet another reductive origin story designed to flatten the history of the gay rights movement into a caricatured narrative about 'trans women.'
bridge.draft: The role of trans women of color in early queer organizing is documented across multiple historians, not just Stryker. This channel covers what the evidence shows. Thanks for watching.
bridge.notes: Dismisses trans women's documented role at Compton's. Response is brief, grounded, cites the broader scholarly record without being defensive.

bridge.video_title: The Nazis Burned 100 Years of Queer Knowledge
bridge.author: @RadicalBastard187
bridge.draft: The people whose work was burned were researchers, patients, and advocates documenting human lives. What was lost in those fires still echoes today. This history matters exactly because we're still in that fight.
bridge.notes: Ambiguous comment on a Nazi book-burning video. Likely means the commenter views Nazis as 'the good guys.' Username suggests bad faith. Drafted bridge works either way. Could also be skipped.

bridge.video_title: The Night Harvey Milk Was Assassinated, a Chorus Sang
bridge.author: @andreworlando7374
bridge.original: Harvey Milk was a pile of s*** he outed the man would save presidents Ford life for his own gain and it screwed that man's life up past the point of no return screw Harvey Milk
bridge.draft: Oliver Sipple's story is real and worth knowing. He saved President Ford's life and paid a painful price for his privacy. His story is part of this history and deserves to be told honestly. The full picture of any historical figure includes the difficult parts.
bridge.notes: Oliver Sipple being outed by Harvey Milk is historically accurate and a real controversy. This response validates the legitimate concern without capitulating to the hostile tone.

skipped.video_title: She Threw Coffee at a Cop... And Started a Revolution!
skipped.author: @debbyvibbert3177
skipped.reason: Pure bad-faith provocation, zero substance. Obama had no connection to the 1966 Compton's Cafeteria event. No good-faith reading possible.

skipped.video_title: Saint and Trans Icon - Joan of Arc
skipped.author: @NicolasChletsos
skipped.original: Slap in the face, the Catholics as slandered person that die for God she's a saint, not icon...
skipped.reason: Incoherent rant with a threat. No substantive argument to respond to.
